_doc: Component-token registry (theme schema v2, Layer 4). Each COMMON component is a small param bundle a theme fills so a 'brutalist card' and a 'porcelain card' differ WITHOUT touching block code. The bundle params map to theme tokens the block CSS consumes via var(--token, <default>) — backward-compatible. `status: wired` means every listed token IS consumed by a block (honesty-tested by tests/test_components.py — this is what caught --card-shadow being authored by 24 themes yet dead); `status: pending` names a component that still needs a block that doesn't exist; `status: n/a` records a component that on inspection is NOT a per-theme axis here (with the reason), so it isn't force-wired for completeness. Theme-specific components (retro-windows OS-chrome, 8-bit pixel-stack, sakura seal) live in the extension registry (compose_extension.py), not here.

card._desc: a styled PANEL — its shadow token carries the most character: brutalist hard-offset (neubrutalism 7px/bauhaus 8px), Swiss inset hairline, terminal phosphor glow, or flat (none). Fill/radius/border come from the surface + shape scale.

image-card._desc: gallery / carousel image tile — corner follows the shape scale, but the drop shadow stays block-tuned (image LIFT is a distinct concern from a styled panel; the schema's card vs img-placeholder split).

bar._desc: data bar (bar chart) — fill is the accent (highlight/mute handled in-block), and the cap radius now follows the shape scale so a sharp theme gets sharp bars and a soft theme rounded ones (vellum r2 vs blue-professional r14, verified).

bullet-marker._desc: the list-item marker glyph — a real per-theme choice, now that the `bullet_list` block exists (built for this): brutalist square (bauhaus/neubrutalism/bold-signal), technical chevron (blueprint/terminal-green/neon-cyber/creative-voltage/aurora-mesh), editorial/elegant em-dash (newsroom/vellum/dark-botanical/…), default dot elsewhere. Set on 19 themes; consumed by .bl-mark::before content.

pill._desc: a pill/tag chip is DEFINITIONALLY fully-round (border-radius:999px = the pill shape); there is no per-theme radius axis to expose. Its fill/ink already resolve from --accent tokens. Not a shape axis → not wired.

timeline-dot._desc: the production `timeline` block is an INTENTIONALLY cinematic dark variant (fixed dramatic look — .tlbg radial dark, accent-ring node); the theme-robust timeline is the archetype SEED (already themed). Tokenising the dark block's dot would fight its deliberate design, so it is left as-is.

counter._desc: the ordinal/counter treatment is already theme-driven upstream — its typography comes from the type-role system (Layer 1) and its big-numeral form from the `background-ordinal` DECORATION (lever #3). No separate component token adds character.
